subject: thank you
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Florida Democratic Party
body:
Friends,

Thank you to everyone who chipped in ahead of our mid-month fundraising deadline. Already, we're putting your gifts to work supporting Annette Taddeo in her bid to flip a state senate seat from red to blue!

That's not all we're doing. **We 're organizing voter registration drives to counteract the hate and division we've seen this week.**

Violent white supremacists tried to intimidate those who stand for justice, equality, and freedom. We're not backing down!

[**Join us at one of our 24 voter registration drives statewide and counteract their hate with our hope. Click here for more information!**](http://act.floridadems.org/page/m/77b4bdb6/1a5213b3/7041de9c/718b655f/2623379248/VEsH/ "http://act.floridadems.org/page/s/rally-to-register-volunteer?utm_medium=email&utm_source=fldems&utm_content=1+-+Join+us+at+one+of+our+24+voter+registrat&utm_campaign=em081817&source=em081817&refcode=em081817")

Thank you again for doing your part to defend the dignity and freedom of all people. Together, we'll defend our shared values and save our country.

Johanna

PS: **[Click here to view a full list of events across the state.](http://act.floridadems.org/page/m/77b4bdb6/1a5213b3/7041de9c/718b655e/2623379248/VEsE/ "http://m.floridadems.org/rallytoregister?utm_medium=email&utm_source=fldems&utm_content=2+-+Click+here+to+view+a+full+list+of+events&utm_campaign=em081817&source=em081817&refcode=em081817")**

The _Cook Political Report_ just ranked Joe's race a "Toss Up". That means this race couldn't be any tighter -- and Republican special interests are sure to take notice and try to buy Indiana's Senate seat.

We have to be ready to fight back with everything we have. Because if we fall behind now, there's a chance we could never recover. And we can't afford to lose. Right now, two well-funded Congressmen with proven records of rubber stamping the Trump agenda are running to replace Joe, and pundits are saying this race could go **EITHER** way.

We might not have their massive GOP war chests behind us, but we have something better: You. **[Will you chip in $5 to Joe's campaign right now and make sure we can win this thing?](http://action.joeforindiana.com/page/m/7f7c27f6/7377f838/378a145d/51fbcd55/248857573/VEsF/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jd-cookrr?refcode=20170817nd&amount=10")**

**Joe is going to have one of the toughest races in the country.** Of the 23 Democrats up for re-election in 2018, Joe is one of the most vulnerable. And with this latest prediction from _Cook_ , it's clearer now than ever before. This is going to bring dark money to Indiana. Not only will the entire weight of the Republican Party be behind whoever wins that primary, special interest groups funded by extremists like the Kochs will get involved, too. **Every time these outside groups see news like this, they see an opportunity to buy another seat.**

**We have to be prepared for whatever they throw at us, and you can help.** Your donation will help us keep our momentum going, fight back, and keep Republicans from replacing Joe with a rubber stamp in the Senate.

name: Tom Perez
subject: BREAKING: Democrats introduce resolution to censure Trump
body:
Republicans must renounce all enablers of white supremacy
---
**It's not enough to reject hatred -- Republicans must denounce Donald Trump for enabling white supremacists. Add your name to call on Republicans to censure Trump:

** **[I'M IN](http://my.democrats. "http://my.democrats.org/Censure-Trump1?keycode=&firstname=Derek&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=20112&utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=1+-+IM+IN&utm_campaign=em_20170818_tp_s2&source=em_20170818_tp_s2")**
Derek --

In the aftermath of Charlottesville, it's become clear that Donald Trump has allowed white supremacists and Nazis to find a home in the Republican Party. So it is not enough for Republicans to simply tweet that they oppose hatred and bigotry -- they must denounce Donald Trump.

Today, House Democrats are introducing a resolution to censure Donald Trump for his defense of the white supremacist mob in Charlottesville.

So, Republicans in Congress have a choice: they can declare their support for our American values, or they can stand with the leader of their party who has embraced white supremacists and given voice to their cause.

**We call on all House Republicans to join with Democrats and censure Donald Trump. Sign your name right now to join the call:**

**[I'M IN](http://my.democrats. "http://my.democrats.org/Censure-Trump1?keycode=&firstname=Derek&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=20112&utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=2+-+IM+IN&utm_campaign=em_20170818_tp_s2&source=em_20170818_tp_s2")**
Thank you,

Tom

Tom Perez
Chair
Democratic National Committee

Paid for by the Democratic National Committee, 430 South Capitol Street SE, Washington DC 20003 and not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee.

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Our email list is the best way we have of staying in regular contact with supporters like you across the country and letting you know about the work Democrats are doing. If you like staying in touch, but want to receive only the most important messages, click [here](http://my.democrats.org/page/m/4052377a/4067b0a/3c8f93021/7e6f2d08/687787231/VEsC/ "https://my.democrats.org/page/signup/less-email?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=4+-+here&utm_campaign=em_20170818_tp_s2&source=em_20170818_tp_s2"). This organization is powered by you, and we'd love to hear your ideas. body:
Friend --

What better way to kick off the weekend than an email all about tax facts? (Seriously though, this is actually Paul's idea of fun).

**Our tax system hasn 't changed since 1986.**

. . . Since then, the Internet became a thing.

. . .You could buy a gallon of gas for $.89.

. . . And Paul was just learning how to drive!

Obviously, a lot has changed in the last 31 years. So why hasn't our tax system? Even with the advent of the Internet and the subsequent overhaul of our world economy, we have the same system we did when Russia was still the USSR.

It's ridiculous,Friend. **Which is why Republicans have a plan to re-work our tax code so that it works for you. **

Want more tax facts? [**Right this way >>**](http://links.targetedvictory.mkt5241.com/ctt?kn=2&ms=MzAyMzY3OTMS1&r=NDY4NTQ0MTA2ODI1S0&b=0&j=MTEwMjExMzMzMAS2&mt=1&rt=0)

subject: Security Upgrades Needed
body:
| |
---
---
| **Derek,**
******One of the first roles I took on as NH GOP Vice-Chair after the January Annual Meeting was chairing the NH GOP Building Committee. Initially, the Committee did an analysis of what improvements needed to be made at HQ, including structural, cosmetic, and security. And, a month or so ago a group of volunteers spent an entire day cleaning the outside of the building, making landscape improvements, and installing an American Flag at the front door. There was a real sense of camaraderie and pride.**
****
******[Yesterday, vandals purposefully trespassed onto State GOP property, spray-painted a hateful message, and lobbed a rock through a first floor window - going right into my office!](http://email.robly.com/wf/click?upn=gT2AZ1ZnBy-2FrecDREa9-2BbR-2BncdYrjYGa-2BcdGvpvk-2BU2jHfQGsLWOHpnnlWbbaErhx-2BdZu6jxfthjHc-2FN7t1N40TB-2BuhhB1igIQ75GU2JmLr5dp9BkLXJBUGKZBhFARUEd9zR3utVpz4K-2Fvkcg6p5vA-3D-3D_enNSH-2FxHAAqXgxiHRFihpX91d75ERAkpfg4XQfmYhCFb47pW8iDRAYLsM9sf9wIa92fG-2FsxU68zXzzMA2nO2VCunl2bm-2FhLXsWS8P3XITs28GUU7Mnlu37POPaWv9myv4YWBbFU9plZi0-2B42tZfBaUujH4mxA3e6jOiyDa5q6HKz45-2B17g6DUNL-2BnIMAR30MtcAzRzIhps3c3tvUtlSiMJKu7bb4JbTltzplErT3SUkHvqy0kh0qYIQ7gj7-2B9bMxGwYEGNNXT1RcRG4jrCI-2FWjce4nPz8UrseScLZAA1grBn5G9IjfO78YjZmbfsozJSk-2F6c4-2BOP7r3uavA7AW-2BEjsxUGew07FjfJwoOAfyrZajjlO3OIHxlqhRmnvWOVKanJ8-2F-2FOSPydC9R-2BqDvQhXBAGcb8pKef3mRzUWMj6-2BC8gK2JF-2BF0m7i1C3onrsy4m3BEGWooQuvWYZL8RZGB4wMBg-3D-3D)

clean_body: | | | Derek, ****One of the first roles I took on as NH GOP Vice-Chair after the January Annual Meeting was chairing the NH GOP Building Committee. Initially, the Committee did an analysis of what improvements needed to be made at HQ, including structural, cosmetic, and security. And, a month or so ago a group of volunteers spent an entire day cleaning the outside of the building, making landscape improvements, and installing an American Flag at the front door. There was a real sense of camaraderie and pride. ** ** ****The staff at NH GOP were quick to get police on site and paint over the hateful message, and we're looking into our options to fix the broken window. It also clear that we need additional security cameras on-site. To upgrade our security and our building, we are going to need some financial support. ** Sincerely, | | Wayne MacDonald New Hampshire GOP Vice-Chair | | | | | | VIDEO COVERAGE OF VANDALISM: | | ** | ---|---|--- Vandalism before being treated by NH GOP Staff NH GOP Staff removed the left-wing message of hate, but more work needs to be done! | This email was sent to mailto:dpwillis67@gmail.com Republican Party of New Hampshire · 10 Water Street · Concord · New Hampshire · 03301 \--UNSUBSCRIBE_ALT--

disclaimer_text: Paid for and Authorized by Debbie Wasserman Schultz for Congress This email was sent to [dpwillis67@gmail
body:
| |
---
| |
---
| Friend,

President Barack Obama implemented the Deferred Action for Childhood Arrivals (DACA) program to protect law-abiding members of our communities who sought a better life. These individuals were brought to our country and have only known America as the country they love.

They live here, work here, contribute to our communities, and many have started families here. They fulfill jobs that are crucial to our communities in fields from health care, to computer science, to emergency services.

Before DACA, millions were living in the shadows, afraid of being deported to a country that they've never known. DACA brought them into the light and America has benefitted as a result. Our country is stronger and our economy is growing thanks to their efforts.

**All of that might end this September.** Republican Attorneys General have given President Trump until September 5th to overturn DACA or else they will sue his administration.

If that happens, more than 750,000 hardworking, law-abiding Americans will face deportation to a country they don't know. Families will be ripped apart. Our country will lose some of our most brilliant minds. Local communities will go without doctors, nurses, engineers, and so much more.

[_**We can 't allow that to happen. Please, sign your name and demand President Trump protect DACA.**_](https://secure.debbiewassermanschultz.com/page/m/10113e28/622b7b5b/55b8a5e/307bdc6a/935950224/VEsH/ "https://petitions.signforgood.com/defendDACA/?code=WassermanSchultz&redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fsecure.actblue.com%2Fdonate%2Fdwse170817%3Frefcode%3Ddwse1707817_m%26recurring%3Dtrue%26amount%3D5")

We can and must do better for our DREAMers. Take action before it's too late.

Thank you,

Debbie |

name: Republican Party of New Hampshire
subject: NH GOP Files a FOIA Request into Governor Hassan's Communication with CMS
body:
PRESS RELEASE

For Immediate Release: August 18, 2017

Contact: Patrick Hynes, 202-321-5310 ( tel:(202)%20321-5310 )

NH GOP Files Freedom of Information Act Request to Federal HHS to
Find Out What Maggie Hassan Knew About Medicaid Scheme and When
She Knew It

CONCORD – Today, the New Hampshire Republican State Committee
filed a Freedom of Information Act (FOIA) request with the
federal Department of Health and Human Services (HHS) to
determine what then-Governor Maggie Hassan knew about the funding
model for the state’s Medicaid expansion costs with hospital
donations, and when she knew it. NH GOP Chair Jeanie Forrester
made the following statement:

“Over the past week, Maggie Hassan’s Medicaid expansion story has
already changed four times. First, she blamed the
administration, then she blamed the Legislature, then, after more
facts came out, she started to acknowledge her role, and now
she’s simply trying to dump the mess she made onto Governor
Sununu. She’s changing her story so quickly that it’s hard to
keep track of all the people she’s tried to shift the
responsibility for leaving a ticking time bomb in state
government.”

“Since it is clear we can’t trust a thing that comes out of
Maggie Hassan’s mouth, it’s high time we get to the bottom of
what she knew about this Medicaid scheme and when she knew it.
Since the consequences of her actions could cost New Hampshire
taxpayers tens of millions of dollars, we are filing this FOIA
request to find out precisely what happened. Maggie Hassan won’t
take responsibility for her actions, so it’s important to take
this step to hold her accountable.”

A copy of the FOIA request can be found here. ( http://email.robly.com/wf/click?upn=3qP3xl6Iy86fMdiFlFopyMu9NHzTR86id69SrjrOv11iDhR8TDN9IOhngUxG9jjvIwXUG0r6YmdAX1W8YlAFOBSwhobRdyLoov-2BRU99l2hA3lBTUTad0-2B9n8P8zJqhwI2PkjFhB3e49HKM1jCcTUVtJDYG0UJVTfcJj-2Fp8YFKP2CIWr4u8lWIZ6XkJhcm6hV_enNSH-2FxHAAqXgxiHRFihpX91d75ERAkpfg4XQfmYhCFb47pW8iDRAYLsM9sf9wIa92fG-2FsxU68zXzzMA2nO2VCunl2bm-2FhLXsWS8P3XITs28GUU7Mnlu37POPaWv9myv4YWBbFU9plZi0-2B42tZfBaUujH4mxA3e6jOiyDa5q6HKz45-2B17g6DUNL-2BnIMAR30MyXYs6Rx9w2aZYLgbJHGIPRDE7M6TqtmqV3vXgzACvb4U9AFL-2F3iBcfHfBC8CluoMDMRnNgwyEV8N4Fv98drhkxHde-2B2vKfn4ULZ8EAWm-2FTjr2xmd-2FbouGXwPz23vxAXlDjJLEVMisM8q1uWbuf9A4GsqG64aMfW2Z-2F9KeRfQiFsHQ2ayHNSCTONrrtMLBj9H8QprRDJ2FSdlM9PpUHeHYkhs-2BT5iOWlPIKuOEKQdwMT6uGSMeiIMKodfPZ-2FjtWMd4ZBjxlWVVAR5E71oxzAzUA-3D-3D )

clean_body: PRESS RELEASE For Immediate Release: August 18, 2017 Contact: Patrick Hynes, 202-321-5310 ( tel:(202)%20321-5310 ) NH GOP Files Freedom of Information Act Request to Federal HHS to Find Out What Maggie Hassan Knew About Medicaid Scheme and When She Knew It CONCORD – Today, the New Hampshire Republican State Committee filed a Freedom of Information Act (FOIA) request with the federal Department of Health and Human Services (HHS) to determine what then-Governor Maggie Hassan knew about the funding model for the state’s Medicaid expansion costs with hospital donations, and when she knew it. NH GOP Chair Jeanie Forrester made the following statement: “Over the past week, Maggie Hassan’s Medicaid expansion story has already changed four times. First, she blamed the administration, then she blamed the Legislature, then, after more facts came out, she started to acknowledge her role, and now she’s simply trying to dump the mess she made onto Governor Sununu. She’s changing her story so quickly that it’s hard to keep track of all the people she’s tried to shift the responsibility for leaving a ticking time bomb in state government.” “Since it is clear we can’t trust a thing that comes out of Maggie Hassan’s mouth, it’s high time we get to the bottom of what she knew about this Medicaid scheme and when she knew it. Since the consequences of her actions could cost New Hampshire taxpayers tens of millions of dollars, we are filing this FOIA request to find out precisely what happened. Maggie Hassan won’t take responsibility for her actions, so it’s important to take this step to hold her accountable.” A copy of the FOIA request can be found here. ( ) BACKGROUND Last Friday, Sen. Hassan blamed the Trump administration for the failures of the Medicaid expansion proposal: “This is a deliberate attempt by the Trump Administration to sabotage New Hampshire’s...Medicaid expansion plan” ( ) By Monday, talking with the Keene Sentinel, Hassan shifted her story to throw blame to Republican legislators: “Republican legislators said to me that they knew that the way they were funding this program would be scrutinized by CMS and would possibly be challenged by CMS, and they said they thought that they had made it as legally defensible as they could” ( ) By Tuesday, Hassan’s staff acknowledged that the funding scheme was always legally dubious: “When CMS during the Obama administration raised concerns that the funding structure constituted a so-called ‘hold harmless’...then-Governor Hassan worked to ensure continued funding for the program” ( ) Finally, by Wednesday, Hassan’s sole focus was on unloading responsibility for her mess onto Governor Sununu: “Senator Hassan expressed to Governor Sununu the need for constant engagement with CMS on a wide range of Medicaid issues, and it is incumbent upon Governor Sununu and Republicans in the Legislature to work with the Trump Administration to ensure that federal funding for the program continues” ( ) footer image

body:
[ Legit ]LegitAction

Friend,

Things have been moving fast since yesterday.

Every member of the President’s Commission on Arts and the Humanities
just resigned, and LegitAction members made enough calls yesterday to
make a major impression at the offices of Democrats on the sham
electoral commission. But the commission’s Democrats still haven’t
announced their resignations.

[ https://act.legitaction.com/sign/votercommission_dunlap/?t=1002&akid=219%2E356062%2EOfxjYE ]We’re asking supporters across the country to call on Democratic
Maine Secretary of State Matthew Dunlap to resign from Trump’s sham
voter commission now. Add your name today.

Thank you for standing up for our democratic legitimacy today,

LegitAction Team

------Original Message------
From: LegitAction Team
Subject: Participation legitimizes white supremacy

Friend,

When CEOs started dropping off President Trump’s economic development
commissions one by one, Trump caved to the pressure and publicly
shuttered the commissions altogether. He had to stop the steady
drip-drip-drip of resignations making him look isolated and small.

We want Trump to feel the same pressure on his fake election
commission.

You already know that the so-called election commission exists to
perpetuate a myth of voter fraud and suppress voter turnout. The
President Trump we’ve seen repeatedly supporting neo-Nazis and white
supremacists, and the Vice President Pence who stands by Trump's every
word, are the same people pushing a sham election commission designed
to find a pretext for disenfranchising voters of color. That’s no
coincidence.

After this week’s events, there can be no more pretense that the sham
election commission is legitimate. All members of the Trump-Pence sham
electoral commission must resign, especially the Democrats. Further
participation in the sham election commission is unacceptable.

[ https://act.legitaction.com/sign/votercommission_dunlap/?t=1003&akid=219%2E356062%2EOfxjYE ]We’re asking supporters across the country to call on Democratic
Maine Secretary of State Matthew Dunlap to resign from Trump’s sham
voter commission now. Add your name today.

There’s virtually no evidence of voter fraud in this country. Many of
the people who insist fraud must be widespread are the same people
intentionally and deliberately trying to take the ability to vote away
from people of color through strict voter ID laws, gerrymandering, and
other modern day Jim Crow laws.

That’s called white supremacy, and anyone who works with Trump to
promote such policies is validating the underlying racist ideology.

In the wake of Trump’s terrible comments following the events in
Charlottesville, business people acted almost immediately to distance
themselves from the White House's support for racism. Many realized
they could not be seen as collaborating with a president and vice
president who support neo-Nazis.

The Trump-Pence sham election commission's efforts are specifically
targeted at people of color and Democratic voters. This commission
needs to be shut down too -- and we need your help to do it.

[ https://act.legitaction.com/sign/votercommission_dunlap/?t=1004&akid=219%2E356062%2EOfxjYE ]Call on Maine Secretary of State Matthew Dunlap to resign from
Trump’s sham voter commission now. Participation is completely
unacceptable.

This phony commission has unequivocally declared war on voters,
particularly voters of color. It is laying the groundwork for racially
motivated voter suppression nationwide. Participation legitimizes
white supremacy. Commission members need to examine their consciences.
It’s past time to pick a side.

Thank you for standing up for our democratic legitimacy today,

body:

**_#NextLevelJobs_**
Governor **Eric Holcomb** this week kicked off a statewide tour with newly minted Secretary of Career Connections & Talent**Blair Milo** , Department of Workforce Development Commissioner **Steve Braun** and Higher Education Commissioner **Teresa Lubbers** to launch [www.NextLevelJobs.org](http://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=5651ca95c3&e=1acd9bb3d6). This next piece of the Governor’s Next Level Agenda will cover the costs of certificates for Hoosiers in high-demand, high-wage fields – and help offset the costs employers in these fields take on when they hire new employees and train them.

In sum total, Next Level Jobs will invest over $24 million over the next two years to support Hoosiers and the businesses that employ them to fill our greatest immediate workforce demands. According to the [_Associated Press_](http://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=3bc9255c8f&e=1acd9bb3d6), this initiative will “help grow the number of welders, machinists, medical assistants and information technology specialists, among others.”

And here’s perhaps the best part: individuals and employers can apply online at [www.NextLevelJobs.org](http://indgop.us11.list-manage2.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=8d601a5ee4&e=1acd9bb3d6) using a simple, five-minute application. And – there’s more – they will receive a response and assistance within 48 hours of applying.

That’s taking government responsiveness to...the Next Level!
\- Kyle

(Photo: Chairman Hupfer addressed the Annual Pike Township GOP Ice Cream Social in Indianapolis on Thursday evening.)

name: Keith Ellison
subject: Every white supremacist must go (add your name)
body:
Racism has no home in the White House.
---
**Tell Trump to fire Seb Gorka, Stephen Miller, and every white supremacist in his administration.

** **[I'M IN](http://my.democrats. "http://my.democrats.org/Fire-White-Supremacists1?keycode=&firstname=Derek&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=20112&utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=1+-+IM+IN&utm_campaign=em_20170818_ke_nd&source=em_20170818_ke_nd")**
Derek --

I don't think it's unreasonable to expect that the most hateful elements of our society won't have a voice at the highest levels of government.

But the fact is that even with Steve Bannon gone, Donald Trump's inner circle still includes prominent white nationalists and enablers of white supremacy.

Seb Gorka, one of Trump's top aides, is a member of a Hungarian Nazi organization. Senior advisor Stephen Miller is a devotee of Richard Spencer, a leader of the so-called alt-right who cheered on Trump's disgraceful response to the events in Charlottesville.

These men are so far below anyone who should be serving in government, and it's beyond disturbing they continue to have the ear of the President of the United States. And as long as they remain among his closest advisors, Trump can never truly reject the hatred that they perpetuate.

Tens of thousands of Democrats signed on to call on Trump to fire Bannon in the past few days -- and today we were successful. Now, we are calling on Donald Trump to fire Gorka, Miller, and anyone else with ties to white supremacy in his administration -- today.

**Add your name to join us:**

**[I'M IN](http://my.democrats. "http://my.democrats.org/Fire-White-Supremacists1?keycode=&firstname=Derek&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=20112&utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=2+-+IM+IN&utm_campaign=em_20170818_ke_nd&source=em_20170818_ke_nd")**
Thank you,

Keith

Keith Ellison
Deputy Chair
Democratic National Committee

**NEW HAMPSHIRE REPUBLICAN PARTY**
**WEEKLY NEWSLETTER**
**FRIDAY, August 18th**
**_A Note From Jeanie_**
**Dear Derek,**
**Summer is almost over and the 2018 election cycle is right around the corner. We’ve already seen candidates for CD1 and CD2 formally announce their intentions to run – and more candidates are likely to announce in the coming weeks. Speaking from personal experience—running for elective office is not a “cake walk.” So a sincere thanks to everyone who steps up to serve – whether at a local, state, or national level.**
****
**With the disappointing performance and deliberate obstructionist behavior by Shea-Porter and Kuster, voters are ready for productive representation in Washington. As an example, instead of trying to find a solution to the 44% increase in health care premiums that our taxpayers will be seeing, Kuster and Shea-Porter are more interested in signing onto a resolution to censure the President.**
****
**Now more than ever, with the destructive antics and the Democratic machine in high gear, our candidates need our help. I encourage you to get involved. Do your homework and choose a candidate to support and then do all you can to help them campaign and win. Give your time, talent, and financial support. Write letters to the editor, host a coffee at your home and invite neighbors and friends, knock on doors, or hold a sign on election day. And, most importantly, get out and vote – because we know that when Republicans vote, Republicans win!**

**Sincerely, **

**Jeanie**
**Join the Grassroots Circle!**

**The Grassroots Circle is a unique way for you to get involved while supporting the Party.**

**REPUBLICAN SPOTLIGHT**
Thank you for all of your submissions! This week's spotlight recognizes all the great Republican dogs in New Hampshire. Here we have Max Freeman from Manchester, Darcy the NH GOP office dog, and Duke. There are countless others, and thanks to Governor Chris Sununu's efforts this week to increase animal protections in New Hampshire they will all be better off. They are all good dogs!

clean_body: nh.gop 8 NEW HAMPSHIRE REPUBLICAN PARTY WEEKLY NEWSLETTER FRIDAY, August 18th A Note From Jeanie Dear Derek, Summer is almost over and the 2018 election cycle is right around the corner. We’ve already seen candidates for CD1 and CD2 formally announce their intentions to run – and more candidates are likely to announce in the coming weeks. Speaking from personal experience—running for elective office is not a “cake walk.” So a sincere thanks to everyone who steps up to serve – whether at a local, state, or national level. ** With the disappointing performance and deliberate obstructionist behavior by Shea-Porter and Kuster, voters are ready for productive representation in Washington. As an example, instead of trying to find a solution to the 44% increase in health care premiums that our taxpayers will be seeing, Kuster and Shea-Porter are more interested in signing onto a resolution to censure the President. Now more than ever, with the destructive antics and the Democratic machine in high gear, our candidates need our help. I encourage you to get involved. Do your homework and choose a candidate to support and then do all you can to help them campaign and win. Give your time, talent, and financial support. Write letters to the editor, host a coffee at your home and invite neighbors and friends, knock on doors, or hold a sign on election day. And, most importantly, get out and vote – because we know that when Republicans vote, Republicans win! Sincerely, Jeanie Join the Grassroots Circle! The Grassroots Circle is a unique way for you to get involved while supporting the Party. Becoming a member couldn't be easier -- join today for as little as $10 per month. There are three levels of support: The Grassroots Supporter, the NHGOP Advocate and the VIP Investor. Intern with the NH GOP! Are you a student or know a student that is interested in Politics? Learn the skills necessary to win elections and meet the Republican leaders that govern our State and write legislation. FEATURED EVENT UPCOMING EVENTS August 23 @ 6:30 pm - 8:30 pm August 30 @ 6:00 pm - 8:00 pm PHOTO OF THE WEEK Our interns had their last week today! Here's a photo of them making s'mores in our parking lot on Monday! REPUBLICAN SPOTLIGHT Thank you for all of your submissions! This week's spotlight recognizes all the great Republican dogs in New Hampshire. Here we have Max Freeman from Manchester, Darcy the NH GOP office dog, and Duke. There are countless others, and thanks to Governor Chris Sununu's efforts this week to increase animal protections in New Hampshire they will all be better off. They are all good dogs!
